title: ficus hirta var. roxburghii
intro: Ficus hirta var. roxburghii, also known as the Roxburgh's fig tree, is a species of plant in the fig family (Moraceae). It is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. It is used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and can be prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil through herbal preparations.
